g.text: runtime/lua/smelt/ (UX layer — the whole user-facing surface)
Rust capabilities (generic, Lua-callable — FFI for intricate logic)
crate: engine (LLM core — frontend-agnostic, zero UI imports)
crate: protocol (wire types — pure data, serde)

«long-lived child:
spawn · send · on_event ·
wait · kill — future primitive
for mcp / bg cmd / etc.»

«all permission policy:
bash AST · pattern match ·
workspace path extract ·
runtime approvals ·
workspace store (JSON)»
Buffer vs Window — what goes where.
Rule of thumb: anything that would *differ* if the
same content were shown in two viewports goes on
Window; anything shared goes on Buffer.
Same Buffer can have 0..N Windows looking at it
with chrome on the Vbox/Hbox itself.
No compound-widget type, no group type — every
composite shape is N×Window inside an Overlay.
no dirty flag. The loop renders after every
event; the compositor diffs current vs previous
Grid and flushes only changed cells. Idle CPU
is zero (loop parks). Things that change over
time (clock, spinner) drive a `Cell` from a
timer; the Cell setter wakes the loop.
